purpose: Push/PR CI for main. Builds release configuration on Windows (VS 2026 / MSVC) and Linux (GCC + Ninja) in parallel; caches each tree to speed up incremental runs. Smoke-test only — does not publish artefacts.
invariants: Windows runner must be 'windows-2025-vs2026' (NOT 'windows-latest'). The VS2026 generator named in CMakePresets.json is only present on the vs2026 runner image; the alias has not yet migrated.
Linux runner is ubuntu-24.04 and installs CMake >=4.2 via lukka/get-cmake because the apt-shipped 3.31 does not satisfy CMakeLists.txt's cmake_minimum_required(4.2).
Linux preset name 'ninja-release' must match an entry in CMakePresets.json#configurePresets — also referenced by release.yml.
JUCE Linux build needs the X11 + ALSA + JACK + Freetype dev headers listed; trimming this list breaks the Linux build.
gotchas: Linux build uses `cmake --build build/ninja-release -j$(nproc)` directly rather than `cmake --build --preset ninja-release` to add the parallel flag. Both work, but if a 'buildPresets' entry adds default parallelism the two paths can drift.

Cache key includes only CMakeLists.txt and CMakePresets.json hashes — a JUCE bump (lib/juce vendored sources) does NOT bust the cache. After updating JUCE, manually invalidate the cache via the Actions UI or rev the cache key here.
